things.name: Romer & Romerberg Old Town
things.description: Beautifully restored medieval old town with the iconic half-timbered Romer City Hall and square

things.name: Stadel Museum
things.description: One of Germany's most important art museums spanning 700 years from Botticelli to Gerhard Richter

things.name: Palmengarten Botanical Garden
things.description: One of Germany's most beautiful botanical gardens with tropical greenhouses and seasonal flower shows

things.name: Goethe House
things.description: Birthplace and family home of Germany's greatest writer, Johann Wolfgang von Goethe, faithfully restored

things.name: Sachsenhausen & Apple Wine District
things.description: Frankfurt's traditional quarter on the south bank — cobblestone streets, apple wine taverns and museums

things.name: Main Tower Observation Deck
things.description: The only Frankfurt skyscraper open to the public — 200-metre views over the financial district skyline

things.name: European Central Bank & Ostend
things.description: The striking twin-towered ECB HQ and the adjacent Ostend neighbourhood with galleries and street food

things.name: Frankfurt Zoo
things.description: One of Europe's oldest and most species-rich zoos founded in 1858 with 4,500 animals

things.name: Museum Embankment (Museumsufer)
things.description: 14 museums along the south bank of the Main — from the Film Museum to the Museum of World Cultures

things.name: Heidelberg Day Trip
things.description: Germany's most romantic city with its famous ruined hilltop castle — 1 hour south by train
